untranslatable_reason: The signal requires detecting confluence between multiple timeframes (intraday pivots vs weekly/monthly pivots) and multiple indicator types (moving averages vs pivots). The allowed indicators list does not include multi-timeframe pivot levels (weekly_pivot, monthly_pivot, r1_weekly, s1_monthly, etc.). Additionally, the rule requires calculating 'tight zones' where two levels from different tools/timeframes are 'close enough' — this zone comparison logic cannot be expressed with the allowed operators (>, <, >=, <=, ==, crosses_above, crosses_below, is). News event catalysts also cannot be quantified in the current indicator set.
translation_notes: To properly implement this signal, the backtest engine would need: (1) Multi-timeframe pivot indicators (weekly_pivot, monthly_pivot, r1_weekly, s1_monthly, etc.); (2) A zone-proximity operator or function to detect when two levels are within a configurable tolerance; (3) News event data integration. The current translation uses single-timeframe pivots as a partial substitute, but this loses the core confluence logic of the original signal.
